title: NIST CSF Detect Function Coverage Gap Analysis

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST CSF v1.1 DE.AE-3 Event data are collected and correlated from multiple sources and sensors ŌĆö UC is the Detect Function coverage-gap analysis, quantifying multi-source event correlation.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST CSF DE.AE-3 (Event data collection and correlation) is enforced ŌĆö Splunk UC-22.7.2: NIST CSF Detect Function Coverage Gap Analysis.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.7.2' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted ŌĆö SME review required.

dataSources: `| inputlookup mitre_attack_all_techniques` (technique_id, technique_name); `| rest /services/saved/searches`; `| inputlookup mitre_user_rule_technique_lookup` (correlation_search_name, technique_id); `` `notable` `` macro (rule_name, annotations.mitre_attack.mitre_attack_id)
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), ES MITRE ATT&CK lookups
spl:
| inputlookup mitre_attack_all_techniques
| fields technique_id, technique_name
| join type=left max=0 technique_id [
    | rest /services/saved/searches splunk_server=local count=0
    | search disabled=0 title="*Correlation Search*"
    | lookup mitre_user_rule_technique_lookup correlation_search_name AS title OUTPUT technique_id
    | stats dc(title) as enabled_rules by technique_id
  ]
| join type=left max=0 technique_id [
    search `notable` earliest=-90d
    | mvexpand annotations.mitre_attack.mitre_attack_id limit=500
    | rename annotations.mitre_attack.mitre_attack_id as technique_id
    | stats dc(rule_name) as rules_with_fires by technique_id
  ]
| fillnull value=0 enabled_rules, rules_with_fires
| eval gap=case(
    enabled_rules=0, "no_mapped_rule",
    enabled_rules>0 AND rules_with_fires=0, "no_recent_notable",
    1=1, "active_signal")
| where gap!="active_signal"
| sort technique_id
| table technique_id, technique_name, enabled_rules, rules_with_fires, gap
description: Highlights MITRE techniques with no mapped correlation search or no recent notable fires, focusing detection engineering on true gaps in the Detect function.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST CSF compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
grandmaExplanation: We keep evidence for Detect Function Coverage Gap Analysis. The NIST Cybersecurity Framework tells us what good practice looks like, and we keep evidence we match it.
implementation: (1) Confirm lookup names on your ES build (`mitre_attack_all_techniques` vs `mitre_attack_techniques`); (2) populate `mitre_user_rule_technique_lookup` (ES documents user mapping of correlation searches to techniques); (3) review quarterly and export gap list to detection engineering backlog.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
ŌĆó Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), ES MITRE ATT&CK lookups.
ŌĆó Ensure the following data sources are available: `| inputlookup mitre_attack_all_techniques` (technique_id, technique_name); `| rest /services/saved/searches`; `| inputlookup mitre_user_rule_technique_lookup` (correlation_search_name, technique_id); `` `notable` `` macro (rule_name, annotations.mitre_attack.mitre_attack_id).
ŌĆó For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ŌĆö Configure data collection
(1) Confirm lookup names on your ES build (`mitre_attack_all_techniques` vs `mitre_attack_techniques`); (2) populate `mitre_user_rule_technique_lookup` (ES documents user mapping of correlation searches to techniques); (3) review quarterly and export gap list to detection engineering backlog.

Step 2 ŌĆö Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup mitre_attack_all_techniques
| fields technique_id, technique_name
| join type=left max=0 technique_id [
    | rest /services/saved/searches splunk_server=local count=0
    | search disabled=0 title="*Correlation Search*"
    | lookup mitre_user_rule_technique_lookup correlation_search_name AS title OUTPUT technique_id
    | stats dc(title) as enabled_rules by technique_id
  ]
| join type=left max=0 technique_id [
    search `notable` earliest=-90d
    | mvexpand annotations.mitre_attack.mitre_attack_id limit=500
    | rename annotations.mitre_attack.mitre_attack_id as technique_id
    | stats dc(rule_name) as rules_with_fires by technique_id
  ]
| fillnull value=0 enabled_rules, rules_with_fires
| eval gap=case(
    enabled_rules=0, "no_mapped_rule",
    enabled_rules>0 AND rules_with_fires=0, "no_recent_notable",
    1=1, "active_signal")
| where gap!="active_signal"
| sort technique_id
| table technique_id, technique_name, enabled_rules, rules_with_fires, gap
```

Understanding this SPL

**NIST CSF Detect Function Coverage Gap Analysis (MITRE ATT&CK)** ŌĆö Highlights MITRE techniques with no mapped correlation search or no recent notable fires, focusing detection engineering on true gaps in the Detect function.

Documented **Data sources**: `| inputlookup mitre_attack_all_techniques` (technique_id, technique_name); `| rest /services/saved/searches`; `| inputlookup mitre_user_rule_technique_lookup` (correlation_search_name, technique_id); `` `notable` `` macro (rule_name, annotations.mitre_attack.mitre_attack_id). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), ES MITRE ATT&CK lookups. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedŌĆörename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

ŌĆó Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
ŌĆó Keeps or drops fields with `fields` to shape columns and size.
ŌĆó Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
ŌĆó Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
ŌĆó Fills null values with `fillnull`.
ŌĆó `eval` defines or adjusts **gap** ŌĆö applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
ŌĆó Filters the current rows with `where gap!="active_signal"` ŌĆö typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
ŌĆó Orders rows with `sort` ŌĆö combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: enabled_rules, gap, rules_with_fires, technique_id, technique_name.

Step 3 ŌĆö Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with ES Incident Review for the relevant correlation. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ŌĆö Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (technique, rules, fires, gap), Column chart (gap counts by category).
visualization: Table (technique, rules, fires, gap), Column chart (gap counts by category).
knownFalsePositives: Detect coverage drops during sensor outages, agent reinstall, or after major reorgs.
